status_detail: INOVYN (INEOS) developing green hydrogen at its Antwerp, Belgium and Rafnes, Norway sites. 20 MW PEM electrolysis at Antwerp site for chemical offtake (supplying chlorine production). Part of INEOS' broader hydrogen strategy for chemical industry decarbonization.

data_management.narrative_summary: INOVYN, the chlor-vinyls subsidiary of INEOS, is developing green hydrogen projects at its chemical production sites in Europe, starting with a 20 MW PEM electrolyser at the Antwerp, Belgium site and additional projects at Rafnes, Norway. The hydrogen will be used as feedstock in INOVYN's chlorine production processes, replacing fossil-derived hydrogen and significantly reducing the carbon footprint of PVC and other chemical products. The Antwerp project is supported by the Flemish government and the European hydrogen ecosystem. INOVYN is uniquely positioned as a vertically integrated hydrogen producer and consumer, with dedicated hydrogen demand from its chemical processes.

power.grid_connection_detail: Grid-connected at INOVYN Antwerp chemical site

sources.title: INOVYN green hydrogen for chemical production — Antwerp project development
